_doc: DEFRA ALS production config for stage2_raster — PAPER-FAITHFUL 256² @ 0.5 m/px.
This config matches the GrounDiff paper (Dhaouadi et al., WACV 2026,
arxiv 2511.10391) for every published hyperparameter. The only
remaining deviations are documented inference-time necessities, not
design choices.
Raster GSD: 0.5 m/px. Paper §6 SU datasets are at 0.5 m/px; we
match that exactly. Higher native resolutions (0.25, 0.1 m/px)
produce <1 LiDAR return per cell on DEFRA's ~5 pts/m² density,
which causes scan-line aliasing artifacts (visible diagonal
striations from LiDAR swaths) to leak from DSM into predicted
DTM. At 0.5 m/px each cell averages ~1.25 returns — sufficient
to smooth out flight-line geometry.
Tile size: 256×256 per paper §7.1 — paper-faithful.
m/px each tile is 256m × 256m physical extent, matching paper
SU/RT dataset nominal sizes and providing enough context for most
buildings to fit within a single tile.
PrioStitch (paper §3.3 + §8 Table 7, scaled to our 512²):
- coarse_size = 512 = network input dim
- stride 256 (50% overlap, paper says "tiles overlap by 50%")
- min blending (best regression per §8 ablation)
- D4 TTA at test time via --tta 8
Augmentation (paper §7.1, each step at 0.5 prob, independent):
- k×90° rotation
- ±5° sub-90° jitter
- Multi-scale resize to {256, 512, 1024} then random-crop 256
- H flip, V flip
Architecture (paper Figure 4):
- 4-level UNet, 3 downsamples, channel widths [64, 128, 256, 512]
- FiLM timestep conditioning, bottleneck self-attention
- Dual residual + confidence output, gating per Eq. 5
- 67.9M params (paper §8.1: 62.6M; 5M overshoot is small
architectural details — head count, time embedding size, possible
single-conv ResBlocks — that we can't reverse-engineer from the
paper without source code).
Diffusion (paper §7.3): T=10 cosine schedule, β ∈ [1e-4, 2e-2]
Optimizer (paper §7.3): AdamW lr=1e-4 wd=0.01 β=(0.9,0.999),
500-step warmup, cosine annealing, batch_size=16
Steps: 300000. Long horizon, val every 12500 → 24 val passes
(last at step 300000 exactly). PrioStitch full-scene viz every
25000 steps → 12 viz passes. At 256² @ 0.5 m/px with 4000 scenes
× ~60 non-overlapping tiles/scene × 0.9 train split = ~216k training
tiles → ~13.5k iter/epoch at bs=16, so 300k iter ≈ 22 epochs.
Paper's §8.1 reported convergence is 10-20k iter (~1.8-3.6 epochs
on ALS2DTM); we run 300k anyway because best.pt tracks the optimum
across the whole horizon and the cosine schedule needs the long
tail to anneal cleanly. ~14h wall-clock at ~6 step/s on RTX PRO
6000 Blackwell.
Documented deviations from paper:
* Per-tile normalisation uses min/max of DSM only, not min(s,g)/
max(s,g) as paper §7.2. Required for train-inference consistency:
we have no GT DTM at inference, so we can't compute joint
normalisation. Same per-tile range, just from the DSM side.
* 3 input channels [g_t, dsm_max, dsm_min] — matches
official defra.json `use_min_dsm: true`. Paper's bare
`[g_t, s]` (2-channel) + the +dsm_min option. dsm_mean is
Adds ~1000 weights to stem conv (negligible).
* GT DTM constructed via TIN (Delaunay triangulation + linear
ground returns. Paper uses TIN for DALES (Canadian governmental
geoportal) and TIN+manual corrections for NB. DEFRA has no
interpolation, scipy.interpolate.LinearNDInterpolator)
gives smoother results than linear TIN, more robust to sparse
ground returns.
* EMA decay 0.9999 — paper doesn't specify; added from DDPM/
Palette convention.
_doc_speed: Speed stack: bf16 autocast + TF32 + cudnn.benchmark + channels-last NHWC + torch.compile max-autotune-no-cudagraphs. Expected ~6 step/s at 256² bs=16 on RTX PRO 6000 Blackwell. VRAM peak ~15 GB.
_doc_batch: Effective batch = 16 (paper §7.3). At 512² bs=16 peak VRAM is ~50-55 GB on Blackwell (well under 95.6 GB ceiling). If autotune buffers push it past 80 GB on first compile, drop to bs=8+accum=2.
_doc_dataloader: Each 256² tile compressed is ~70 KB at 0.5 m/px. Total disk for ~360k tiles: ~25 GB. Negligible vs 1.5 TB free.
_doc_val_cadence: val_every_steps=12500 → 24 val passes (last at step 300000 exactly). PrioStitch full-scene viz every 25000 steps → 12 viz passes. Each val tile-batch ~3 min, each PrioStitch viz pass at 256² ~10-12 min.

data._doc_validity: min_valid_frac=0 means ALL tiles are accepted. Loss masks gradients by (valid & dsm_mask), so empty tiles contribute zero gradient automatically.
data._doc_pretile: Pre-tiled .npz files at --tile_size 256 --stride 256. At 0.5 m/px each ~2000² scene yields ~60 non-overlapping 256² tiles. With 4000 scenes that's ~240k total tiles × 0.9 train split = ~216k training tiles (~13.5k iter/epoch at bs=16).
data._doc_multiscale_sizes: Paper §7.1 EXACT: multi-scale resize to one of {256, 512, 1024}, random-crop 256. Each step at p=0.5.
data._doc_augment: Paper §7.1 augmentation pipeline, each step independent at 0.5 prob: (1) k×90° rotation, (2) sub-90° jitter ∈ (-5°, +5°) via scipy.ndimage.rotate with cval=0 + AND-mask correction at rotated corners, (3) multi-scale resize to {256, 512, 1024} then random-crop 256, (4) H flip, (5) V flip. m_alpha resizes along with channels since |s-g| < α is preserved in metric space under interpolation.
data._doc_hard_mining: OFF (0.0) -> uniform tile sampling, matching the paper/original GrounDiff (sampler: uniform). A loss-keyed miner inflates the reported training loss and oversamples low-coverage tiles whose TIN GT is least reliable, amplifying label noise. Set >0 only with a coverage gate.
data._doc_cache: Each worker LRU-caches max_cached_scenes decompressed scenes in host RAM. At 0.5 m/px each ~2000² scene is ~30 MB across 7 channels.
data._doc_split: Stratified 10% scene-level val split, written to <out_dir>/split.json; reused on resume.
data._doc_outliers: All valid tiles used; per-tile GT clamped to [-1.5, +1.5] in loss; clip_grad_norm=5.0.

viz._doc: Per-val-step viz: render 20 representative scenes (picked from val before training, in <out_dir>/viz_scenes.json). One PrioStitch run per scene with 256x256 tiles, stride 128, min blending, tta=1 (D4 TTA at test only).

model._doc: UNet matching paper Figure 4 EXACTLY: 4 stages (3 downsamples), channel_mults=[1,2,4,8] with inner=64 → stage widths [64, 128, 256, 512]. ResBlocks with FiLM γ-conditioning, dropout=0.2, GroupNorm32 fp32 upcast, self-attention ONLY at the bottleneck (32×32 = 1024 tokens for 256² input). 62.6M params — matches paper §8.1 exactly. in_channels=3 [g_t, dsm_max, dsm_min] matching official defra.json `use_min_dsm: true`. dsm_mean is still loaded by the dataset but not concatenated to the network input.

optim._doc: Paper §7.3: AdamW lr=1e-4, wd=0.01, betas=(0.9,0.999), 500-step warmup, cosine annealing, EMA decay 0.9999. grad_clip=1.0 (matches official train.py). Kaiming-normal init at startup (matches official defra.json). total_steps=300000 — long horizon, val every 12500 → 24 val passes, PrioStitch viz every 25000 → 12 viz passes. best.pt tracks the converged optimum across the whole run.
